title: Wulfenia houghtoniana
taxonomy: Wulfenia houghtoniana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It belongs to the genus Wulfenia and is a native species found in the Himalayas. The plant is further classified as Wulfenia houghtoniana in the species level. It is a rare and endangered species due to its limited geographical range and habitat destruction.

intro: Wulfenia houghtoniana is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as alpine wulfenia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. The bioactive constituents of Wulfenia houghtoniana include iridoid glycosides, specifically wulfenieoside and wulfenianoside, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
